title: Matrix Breadth-First Search
description: Given an m×n grid of 0s and 1s, find the length of the shortest path from top-left to bottom-right. You can move in 4 directions through cells with value 0. Return -1 if no path exists.
key_concept: BFS guarantees shortest path in unweighted graphs. Level-by-level expansion — first time you reach destination is shortest path.
when_best: Shortest path in unweighted graphs, level-order problems, minimum steps

hints.L1: BFS with a queue. Start from (0,0) with distance 1. For each cell, add unvisited valid neighbors with distance+1.
hints.L2: Use a deque. Track visited set. Dequeue cell, check if it's the target, enqueue 4 neighbors if valid and unvisited.
hints.L3: queue=deque([(0,0,1)]); visited={(0,0)}; while queue: r,c,dist=popleft(); if r==rows-1 and c==cols-1: return dist; for nr,nc in neighbors: if valid and not visited: visited.add; queue.append((nr,nc,dist+1))
